best_practice_2025_2026.name: Beyond Majority Voting: Higher-Order Aggregation (Optimal Weight / Inverse Surprising Popularity)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Aggregate multiple source answers by assigning DIFFERENTIATED weights from first- AND second-order information (latent heterogeneity + correlation across sources), provably beating majority voting. The SOTA primitive for 'weight corroborators, then compute weighted consensus over a basket' that models partial (non-copy) correlation as a continuous down-weight.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: ICML 2026 accepted; provable improvement over majority voting under mild assumptions; directly upgrades POLARIS's BINARY copy-or-origin independence to a continuous correlation-aware weight. Verified title/authors/date against arXiv abstract.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: arXiv nonexclusive-distrib 1.0 (no code release; reimplement from paper)

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Reliability-aware iterative truth discovery (TruthFinder / CRH family)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Jointly estimate per-source reliability AND the consensus truth: a source agreeing with high-weight sources gains weight; a value claimed by high-weight sources is more likely truth. The canonical frame for source-reliability-aware weighted aggregation over conflicting multi-source claims.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: Still the cited foundation in 2025 evidence-aggregation work; gives the weight<->consensus feedback POLARIS lacks (its weight is a fixed authority prior, consensus a raw count). Implemented today in crowd-kit (Dawid-Skene/M-MSR).
best_practice_2025_2026.date: 2015 survey, foundation still in active 2025 use

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Confidence-Weighted-Consensus aggregation (Roundtable Policy)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Combine multiple sources/agents weighted by each source's CONFIDENCE into a consensus, instead of flat majority/count — the multi-agent analogue of weighting a claim basket by per-member credibility.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: 2025; demonstrates confidence-weighting beats unweighted consensus on multi-agent reasoning; maps onto replacing POLARIS's verified_support_origin_count (a count) with a credibility-weighted strength. Verified date+license via primary PDF.
best_practice_2025_2026.license: CC-BY-4.0 (paper); code license unverified — pattern-inspiration only

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Source-level disagreement quantification for claim verification (Contradiction to Consensus)
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Retrieve evidence for BOTH a claim and its NEGATION across diverse sources (Wikipedia/PubMed/Google), aggregate a unified KB, and QUANTIFY + visualize inter-source disagreement via model confidence — turning 'contested' from a boolean into a calibrated spread.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: 2026; exactly POLARIS's clinical multi-source-with-disagreement use case; upgrades the boolean refuter-edge 'contested' basket_verdict to a quantified disagreement signal for the disclosure layer. Verified title/date via arXiv.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: CRED-1 open multi-signal domain credibility dataset
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Offline 2,672-domain transparent composite credibility table (0.0-0.962) from 5 auditable signals (category 50%, Iffy.news 15%, fact-check frequency 15%, Tranco 5%, domain age 5%). Sovereign, no NewsGuard/MBFC API.
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: 2026; the open/transparent/offline alternative to proprietary credibility services — gives POLARIS's news/web/social tiers a deterministic auditable weight (the §-1.3 'tier=weighting, social stays low-weight' DNA). Verified license (CC BY 4.0), signals, GitHub aloth/cred-1 + Zenodo via primary HTML.

best_practice_2025_2026.name: Bayesian Beta-Binomial posterior confidence over a basket
best_practice_2025_2026.what: Combine per-source agreement evidence with source-reliability WEIGHTS into a Beta posterior so a claim's confidence is a calibrated probability + credible interval; unreliable sources are naturally down-weighted in the posterior update (mixture-prior reliability aggregation).
best_practice_2025_2026.why_sota: 2026 multi-fidelity Bayesian updating; the principled replacement for POLARIS's log1p count score (corroboration.compute_signal_d) — yields a CALIBRATED basket confidence that the existing calibration_metrics.py (Brier/ECE) can then score in production.

open_source_solutions.name: crowd-kit
open_source_solutions.what_it_does: Mature truth-discovery / label-aggregation toolkit: Dawid-Skene, One-coin D-S, GLAD, M-MSR, Wawa, ZeroBasedSkill — the source-reliability-aware weighted-aggregation algorithms for deciding a consensus value over multiple noisy sources with per-source reliability estimation. scikit-learn fit_predict API; JOSS-published 2024. Maps onto POLARIS baskets: members=sources, claim value=label, credibility=worker reliability.

open_source_solutions.name: CRED-1 domain-credibility dataset
open_source_solutions.what_it_does: Offline 2,672-domain transparent composite credibility table (0.0-0.962) from 5 auditable signals. Drop-in deterministic credibility WEIGHT for news/web/social tiers (the non-journal tiers POLARIS authority priors handle weakest), with no NewsGuard/MBFC API dependency — fits the sovereign 'weight don't filter, social stays low-weight' DNA.

open_source_solutions.name: Roundtable Policy reference impl
open_source_solutions.what_it_does: Confidence-weighted-consensus aggregation recipe — combine per-source outputs weighted by per-source confidence rather than a flat majority/count. Pattern source for replacing POLARIS's verified_support_origin_count with a credibility-weighted consensus strength.
open_source_solutions.license: Paper CC-BY-4.0; code license unverified — treat as pattern-inspiration, do not vendor blind

open_source_solutions.name: Higher-Order Aggregation (OW / ISP)
open_source_solutions.repo_or_pip: arXiv:2510.01499 (ICML 2026) — no public repo found at fetch time
open_source_solutions.what_it_does: Optimal Weight + Inverse Surprising Popularity: provably-better-than-majority aggregation modeling latent source correlation + heterogeneity as a continuous weight. Small deterministic algorithm to reimplement to make POLARIS independence handling continuous instead of binary copy/origin.
open_source_solutions.license: arXiv nonexclusive-distrib 1.0 (no code license — REIMPLEMENT from the paper)

open_source_solutions.name: Trialstreamer / RobotReviewer (clinical lane reference)
open_source_solutions.what_it_does: Clinical evidence-aggregation precedent: Cochrane RoB-2-style per-study risk weighting + PICO/effect extraction across multiple trials — the domain-correct analogue of credibility-weighting in the clinical tier (GRADE-adjacent). Reference for weighting clinical corroborators by study quality, not just host independence.
open_source_solutions.license: RobotReviewer GPL-3.0 (copyleft — pattern/reference ONLY, do not link into the sovereign binary)

open_source_solutions.name: scikit-learn (calibration backbone)
open_source_solutions.what_it_does: CalibratedClassifierCV / calibration_curve + Brier — the production backbone to WIRE POLARIS's already-built calibration_metrics.py (Brier/ECE/MCE) into a live basket-confidence calibration loop (currently offline-only, no production caller).

facets.current_polaris: POLARIS has a deep, well-architected corroboration/weighting stack (the §-1.3 weight-and-consolidate DNA is real code), but it weights by FIXED authority priors and aggregates by a COUNT of independent origins — no reliability-feedback, no correlation continuum, no calibrated posterior. Grounded: (1) synthesis/weight_mass.py:160-180 — cluster_mass = authority_score(canonical_origin) ONLY; copies contribute ZERO (copy-uninflatable 'vax-defense', KBT arXiv:1502.03519); credibility_weight is carried but DELIBERATELY NOT a mass factor to preserve the no-inflation invariant; ADVISORY, default-OFF PG_SWEEP_WEIGHT_MASS. (2) synthesis/independence_collapse.py:258-299,330-429 — pure-Python TF-IDF cosine ≥0.85 + eTLD+1 host-collapse cluster sources into independent origins; canonical = earliest-dated else lowest-authority (copy-invariance). Correlation is BINARY (copy vs origin), not continuous. (3) synthesis/credibility_pass.py:209-233,611-762 — per-claim ClaimBasket keeps ALL supporting_members (never drops, §-1.3); verified_support_origin_count (the only strengthening count) = DISTINCT origin clusters whose member passed ISOLATED per-member span verify (_verify_member_in_isolation:341-388, anti-laundering single-token); basket_verdict is a LABEL (full/partial/contested/unverified, :161-164) from counts + refuter edges, never resurrects a dropped sentence. (4) synthesis/finding_dedup.py:455-546,696-720 — corroboration_count = count_independent_hosts (registrable domains) per numeric finding, with same-work (DOI/title) consolidation so N URLs of one paper = ONE origin. (5) authority/corroboration.py:67-80 — compute_signal_d turns corroboration_count into a log1p sub-score (closest thing to a consensus-confidence, but a monotone count transform, not agreement- or reliability-weighted). (6) authority/credibility_skill.py:106-116,176-182 — LLM credibility judge -> reliability_score/credibility_weight capped at clamp01(authority_score)+uplift (anti-fabrication). (7) synthesis/calibration_metrics.py:1-77 — Brier/ECE/MCE/reliability-bins EXIST but pure offline, NO production caller. The frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify/NLI/4-role/provenance) is the only hard gate and is untouched — everything above is ADVISORY weight/label.
facets.gap_vs_frontier: Five concrete gaps, none requiring touching the frozen faithfulness engine (all ADVISORY weight/consensus/confidence layers): (1) NO reliability-aware truth-discovery — POLARIS source weight = a fixed authority prior; the frontier (truth-discovery survey 1505.02463; crowd-kit Dawid-Skene/M-MSR) iterates weight<->consensus so a source's weight reflects its AGREEMENT with other credible sources, not just its venue. (2) Correlation is BINARY — independence_collapse is copy-or-origin (cosine≥0.85); 'Beyond Majority Voting' OW/ISP (2510.01499, ICML 2026) weight by a CONTINUOUS latent correlation, so two partially-redundant non-copy sources are partially down-weighted, where POLARIS counts them as two full independent origins. (3) Consensus strength is a COUNT, not a credibility-weighted aggregate — verified_support_origin_count and compute_signal_d are origin counts / log1p of a count; Roundtable (2509.16839) + confidence-weighted consensus would weight each corroborator by its credibility_weight (which POLARIS computes but then refuses to fold into the strength signal). (4) NO calibrated basket confidence — no Beta-Binomial / Bayesian posterior turning a basket into a calibrated probability + credible interval; basket_verdict is a 4-value label. (5) calibration_metrics.py is UNWIRED — the Brier/ECE machinery that would PROVE the basket confidences are calibrated has no production caller. Disagreement (refuter edges -> 'contested') is boolean, not the quantified source-level-disagreement spread of 2602.18693. Sovereignty: POLARIS's authority priors lean journal/gov; CRED-1 (CC BY 4.0) gives the news/web/social tiers a transparent offline credibility weight without a NewsGuard/MBFC API.
license_flags: RobotReviewer — GPL-3.0 (copyleft): reference/pattern ONLY; do NOT link into the sovereign binary or it forces GPL on POLARIS.

Roundtable Policy (arXiv:2509.16839) — paper CC-BY-4.0 but NO code license verified at fetch time: pattern-inspiration only per the frontier-tech-only / unverified-external-runtime rule, do not vendor blind.
Beyond Majority Voting OW/ISP (arXiv:2510.01499) — arXiv nonexclusive-distrib 1.0, no code release found: REIMPLEMENT from the paper (algorithm small/deterministic).
NewsGuard / MBFC API — PROPRIETARY / closed / sovereignty-blocking: avoid; CRED-1 (CC BY 4.0) is the open substitute.
Trialstreamer/RobotReviewer also depend on external MEDLINE services — sovereignty check before any live wiring (clinical lane runs offline/sovereign).
Sovereign-OK: crowd-kit (Apache-2.0), scikit-learn (BSD-3), CRED-1 (CC BY 4.0, attribution only).
bakeoff_candidates: crowd-kit Dawid-Skene / M-MSR (Apache-2.0) — reliability-aware weighted aggregation over a basket: estimate per-source reliability from cross-source agreement and produce a credibility-weighted consensus value + per-source weight, replacing the flat independent-origin COUNT. Bench vs current count-based verified_support_origin_count on a banked corpus_snapshot.json (replay-harness, §-1.4) for a more discriminative/calibrated strength WITHOUT relaxing the frozen verify gate.

Beyond-Majority-Voting OW / ISP (reimplemented from arXiv:2510.01499) — continuous correlation-aware weighting to upgrade independence_collapse from binary copy/origin to a graded down-weight of partially-redundant sources. Bench: does it change which baskets read 'multi-source corroborated (>=2 verified origins)' on the banked corpus?

CRED-1 (CC BY 4.0) domain-credibility table — deterministic offline credibility weight for news/web/social tiers vs the current LLM-judge+authority-prior. Bench: agreement with tier_classifier authority_score on overlapping domains + whether it sharpens low-tier down-weighting.

Beta-Binomial / mixture-prior Bayesian basket confidence (scikit-learn calibration backbone, BSD-3) — turn each basket into a calibrated posterior probability + credible interval, weighting corroborators by credibility_weight. Bench against offline calibration_metrics.py Brier/ECE on §-1.1-audited claims (1=held up, 0=did not) to PROVE calibration beats compute_signal_d's log1p count.

Source-level-disagreement quantifier (pattern from arXiv:2602.18693) — turn the boolean 'contested' basket_verdict into a quantified inter-source confidence spread for the disclosure layer; clinical-domain-appropriate (claim + negated-claim retrieval partly present via refuter edges).

A key honesty issue: the prompt promises "7 facet researches" but the array contains exactly **one** facet (`multisource_corroboration_weighting`). I must not fabricate six facets I wasn't given. I'll structure the report per the requested skeleton, be fully honest that only one of seven facets was supplied, and make the per-facet sections accordingly scoped. Let me write the report. The task says return findings directly as the final message (not a .md file), so I'll output the report as my final response.

The report is below.

---

# POLARIS Consolidation & Baskets — Landscape Report (I-cons-001 #1295)

## Section 0 — How this is grounded, and a hard honesty flag up front

This report synthesizes the facet researches supplied for I-cons-001. One material fact first: the brief said seven facet researches would be attached, but the payload contained exactly one facet — `multisource_corroboration_weighting`. I will not invent the other six. Sections 1, 2, 4, and 5 are written from the one facet that exists, plus the load-bearing POLARIS code I verified myself. Where a section calls for the missing six facets (single-claim atomization, claim clustering, conflict detection, set-level verification, synthesis, and whatever the seventh was meant to be), I name the gap plainly instead of filling it with guesswork. The pipeline in Section 2 is still drawn end-to-end, but the steps the missing facets would have covered are labeled as "not yet researched" rather than backed by a named 2025/2026 source.

Every claim I make about the *current* POLARIS code was checked against the actual files in this repo, not taken on the facet's word. The files I read: `src/polaris_graph/synthesis/weight_mass.py`, `independence_collapse.py`, `credibility_pass.py`, `finding_dedup.py`, `calibration_metrics.py`; `src/polaris_graph/authority/corroboration.py`, `credibility_skill.py`; and the consumers in `src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py` and `provenance_generator.py`.

One correction to the facet research, found during that check: the facet said the corroboration/weighting layers are "ADVISORY, default-OFF." That is true for `PG_SWEEP_WEIGHT_MASS` (it has no production caller — pure library, default off, confirmed at `weight_mass.py:12,36,41`). It is **not** true for the credibility pass. `PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN` defaults to **on** (`multi_section_generator.py:283` and `:7088` both treat unset as "on"), so `credibility_pass.py`, `verified_support_origin_count`, and `basket_verdict` are live on the production run path, not dormant. They are advisory in the sense that they label and weight but never drop a sentence or flip release — but they do run on every paid run. I corrected this throughout.

The external 2025/2026 sources below are repeated from the facet research with their stated dates and licenses; I did not independently re-fetch the arXiv papers, so I carry the facet's own caveats forward verbatim (for example, the unverified code licenses) rather than upgrading any claim's confidence.

## Section 1 — Per-facet 2025/2026 best-practice recipe table

Only one facet was supplied. The table is therefore one row deep, fully populated. The other six facets are listed as blanks so the operator can hear exactly what is missing.

| Facet | 2025/2026 best-practice recipe (named tool + license + role) |
|---|---|
| **Multi-source corroboration & weighting** | Reliability-aware weighted aggregation over a claim basket. Named pieces: **crowd-kit** (Apache-2.0) for Dawid-Skene / M-MSR truth-discovery — role: estimate per-source reliability from cross-source agreement and produce a credibility-weighted consensus value. **CRED-1** (CC BY 4.0) 2,672-domain offline credibility table — role: deterministic sovereign credibility weight for news/web/social tiers. **scikit-learn** (BSD-3) — role: production calibration backbone (Brier/ECE) to wire the existing offline calibration code into a live loop. Reimplement-from-paper algorithms: **Optimal Weight / Inverse Surprising Popularity** from "Beyond Majority Voting" (arXiv:2510.01499, ICML 2026, arXiv license, no code) — role: continuous correlation-aware down-weighting; and **Beta-Binomial / mixture-prior Bayesian posterior** (arXiv:2601.13396, arXiv license) — role: calibrated basket confidence with a credible interval. Pattern-only references (do not vendor): **Roundtable confidence-weighted consensus** (arXiv:2509.16839, paper CC-BY-4.0, code license unverified); **source-level disagreement quantification** (arXiv:2602.18693, arXiv license) for turning boolean "contested" into a calibrated spread; **RobotReviewer / Trialstreamer** (GPL-3.0 — reference only, copyleft) for the clinical study-quality weighting analogue. |
| Single-claim atomization | NOT RESEARCHED — facet not supplied. |
| Claim clustering / grouping into baskets | NOT RESEARCHED — facet not supplied. POLARIS already does this in code (see Section 3), but no 2025/2026 best-practice source was provided to benchmark it against. |
| Conflict / contradiction detection | NOT RESEARCHED — facet not supplied. The corroboration facet touches this only at the edge (the disagreement-quantification paper). |
| Set-level / basket verification | NOT RESEARCHED — facet not supplied. |
| Synthesis / composition from baskets | NOT RESEARCHED — facet not supplied. |
| (Seventh facet) | UNKNOWN — not named anywhere in the payload. |

## Section 2 — The basket pipeline, end to end

This is the ordered chain a basket pipeline runs, with each step mapped to the 2025/2026 state of the art and the open-source pick. Where the facet that covers a step was not supplied, I say so. The chain is: atomic claims, then clustering into baskets, then corroboration and weighting, then conflict detection, then set-level verification, then synthesis.

**Step 1 — Atomic claims.** Break each generated or retrieved statement into one checkable claim. State of the art: not researched (facet missing). POLARIS today produces provenance-tokened sentences and per-finding rows; that is its atomization seam, but there is no supplied frontier source to compare it to.

**Step 2 — Cluster claims into baskets.** Group sources that carry the same claim into one basket; group same-work copies of one paper into one origin. State of the art for the clustering method itself: not researched (facet missing). What *is* researched is what happens to the members once clustered, which is steps 3 onward. POLARIS does the clustering today with TF-IDF cosine plus host-collapse (Section 3).

**Step 3 — Corroborate and weight the basket.** This is the one fully-researched step. Two sub-decisions: how much each member is worth, and how correlated the members are.
- For member worth: the SOTA is reliability-aware truth discovery — a source's weight reflects how much it agrees with other credible sources, not just its venue prior. OSS pick: **crowd-kit** Dawid-Skene / M-MSR (Apache-2.0). For the credibility prior on non-journal domains, the OSS pick is the **CRED-1** offline table (CC BY 4.0).
- For correlation: the SOTA is continuous down-weighting of partially-redundant sources, from "Beyond Majority Voting" Optimal Weight / Inverse Surprising Popularity (arXiv:2510.01499). No OSS release; reimplement from the paper.
- For the resulting confidence number: the SOTA is a Bayesian Beta-Binomial posterior giving a calibrated probability plus a credible interval (arXiv:2601.13396), with **scikit-learn** (BSD-3) as the calibration backbone.

**Step 4 — Detect conflict.** Flag when basket members disagree. State of the art at the edge of the corroboration facet: quantify the disagreement as a calibrated spread rather than a boolean, retrieving evidence for both the claim and its negation (arXiv:2602.18693). Full conflict-detection facet: not supplied. OSS pick: none named that is drop-in; the paper is pattern-only.

**Step 5 — Verify the set.** Decide the basket's faithfulness against its whole set of members, not one span. State of the art: not researched as its own facet. POLARIS's frozen faithfulness engine occupies this slot and is out of scope to change.

**Step 6 — Synthesize.** Compose the report from verified baskets. State of the art: not researched (facet missing).

The honest read of this pipeline: of six steps, only Step 3 has a supplied 2025/2026 SOTA and OSS pick. The other five are either already implemented in POLARIS without a frontier benchmark, or frozen, or simply not researched yet. The operator should commission the missing six facets before treating this as a complete pipeline design.

## Section 3 — KEEP vs ADD/FIX against current POLARIS

This is the honest accounting against the weight-and-consolidate DNA, with the faithfulness engine frozen. Every code claim here I verified in the repo.

**KEEP — these are correct and match the DNA, do not touch them.**

1. Keep the keep-all basket. `credibility_pass.py` keeps every supporting member and never drops one; `basket_verdict` is a label (full / partial / contested / unverified at `:161-164`), never a gate that resurrects or kills a sentence. This is the consolidate-don't-drop DNA, correctly built.
2. Keep the copy-uninflatable weight mass. `weight_mass.py:160-180` sets cluster mass to the canonical origin's authority score only; copies contribute zero, and `credibility_weight` is carried as a disclosed field but deliberately kept out of the mass to preserve the no-inflation invariant. The in-code comment (`:161-167`) explains exactly why folding credibility into mass would let adding a copy raise the score. That reasoning is sound. Keep it.
3. Keep the same-work consolidation. `finding_dedup.py` collapses N URLs of one paper (same DOI, else same folded title plus a discriminator) into one origin so they stop inflating `corroboration_count` (`:66-122`, `:158-175`). This is the right anti-double-count behavior.
4. Keep the anti-laundering isolated verify. `_verify_member_in_isolation` (`credibility_pass.py:341-388`) verifies each member against its own single span with exactly one provenance token, so a member whose own span lacks the claim's number or content fails alone even if a multi-citation union would pass. This can only undercount, never inflate. Keep it.
5. Keep the frozen faithfulness engine untouched. strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance stay the only hard gate. Nothing in this report's recommendations requires touching it; every recommendation lives in the advisory weight, consensus, and confidence layers.

**ADD / FIX — five concrete gaps, all in advisory layers, none touching the frozen engine.**

1. Source weight is a fixed authority prior with no reliability feedback. `credibility_skill.py` produces a credibility judgment capped at `clamp01(authority) + uplift` (`:224-229`), and the priors-only fallback is just the authority score (`:173-187`). There is no loop where a source's weight reflects its agreement with other credible sources. ADD: a reliability-aware aggregation step using crowd-kit Dawid-Skene / M-MSR, benchmarked against the current count.
2. Correlation is binary. `independence_collapse.py` clusters on TF-IDF cosine at or above 0.85 (`:86`) plus host-collapse — a source is either a copy or a full independent origin, nothing in between. ADD: continuous down-weighting (Optimal Weight / ISP, reimplemented) so two partially-redundant non-copy sources count as less than two full origins.
3. Consensus strength is a count, not a credibility-weighted aggregate. `verified_support_origin_count` is a count of distinct verified origins; `corroboration.compute_signal_d` (`:67-80`) is just `log1p` of that count. The credibility weight POLARIS already computes is not folded into the strength signal. FIX: weight each corroborator by its credibility when computing strength, per the confidence-weighted-consensus pattern (pattern-only source; do not vendor blind).
4. No calibrated basket confidence. `basket_verdict` is a four-value label, not a probability. ADD: a Beta-Binomial posterior that turns a basket into a calibrated probability plus a credible interval.
5. The calibration machinery is built but unwired. `calibration_metrics.py` implements Brier / ECE / MCE / reliability bins (`:1-12`, `:79-127`) but I confirmed by grep that nothing else in `src/` calls `calibration_report`, `brier_score`, or `reliability_bins` — it is the only file referencing them. FIX: wire it to a live caller (scikit-learn backbone) so the basket confidences can actually be proven calibrated.

One DNA caution on the ADD items: any reliability-feedback or correlation down-weight that *lowers* a basket's strength must be a disclosed weight, never a drop. The day-waster anti-pattern (caps, targets, thinners to hit a breadth number) is banned. These additions earn their place only if they make the strength signal more discriminative while still keeping every member in the basket.

## Section 4 — Bake-off candidate list per facet

OSS-first. Acceptance is behavioral — the effect must appear in real rendered output on a banked `corpus_snapshot.json` via the replay harness, never a vendor score or a green test. Only the one supplied facet has candidates; the rest are blank by honesty.

**Multi-source corroboration & weighting:**

1. **crowd-kit Dawid-Skene / M-MSR** (Apache-2.0). Test: replace the flat independent-origin count with a reliability-aware weighted consensus on a banked corpus; acceptance is a measurably more discriminative or better-calibrated strength signal with the frozen verify gate untouched.
2. **Optimal Weight / ISP, reimplemented from arXiv:2510.01499** (arXiv license, no code — reimplement). Test: does the continuous correlation down-weight change which baskets read as multi-source corroborated (two or more verified origins) on the banked corpus?
3. **CRED-1 domain-credibility table** (CC BY 4.0). Test: agreement with the current `tier_classifier` authority score on overlapping domains, and whether it sharpens the down-weighting of low tiers (the DNA's "social stays low-weight").
4. **Beta-Binomial / mixture-prior basket confidence** (scikit-learn backbone, BSD-3). Test: score it against the offline `calibration_metrics.py` Brier / ECE on §-1.1-audited claims (1 = held up, 0 = did not) and prove calibration beats the `log1p` count.
5. **Source-level disagreement quantifier, pattern from arXiv:2602.18693** (arXiv license, pattern-only). Test: turn the boolean "contested" verdict into a quantified spread for the disclosure layer; clinical-appropriate because refuter edges already give partial claim-plus-negation coverage.

**All other facets (atomization, clustering, conflict detection, set verification, synthesis, seventh):** no bake-off candidates — facets not supplied. Commission them first.

## Section 5 — Honest uncertainty and license flags

**Uncertainty.**
- Six of seven facets were not supplied. This report is one-seventh of the landscape it was meant to be. Treat Sections 2 and 4 as scaffolds, not complete designs.
- I did not re-fetch the arXiv papers. Dates, claims, and licenses for the external sources are carried from the facet research, with the facet's own caveats intact. Two of the named papers (2510.01499 OW/ISP, 2601.13396 Beta-Binomial) and one dataset (2604.20856 CRED-1) have arXiv-format IDs that I could not independently confirm resolve; the facet asserts it verified them against primary sources, but I am flagging that I did not.
- The facet's "ADVISORY, default-OFF" framing was wrong for the credibility pass; I corrected it to default-on after reading the code. If other facet claims share that optimism, re-verify before building.
- Whether any ADD item actually improves output is a hypothesis until it passes the replay harness on a banked corpus and a §-1.1 line-by-line audit. Green tests and vendor scores do not count.

**License flags.**
- **RobotReviewer / Trialstreamer — GPL-3.0 (copyleft).** Reference and pattern only. Do not link into the sovereign binary; linking forces GPL on POLARIS. Both also depend on external MEDLINE services — a sovereignty check is required before any live wiring.
- **Roundtable confidence-weighted consensus (arXiv:2509.16839) — paper CC-BY-4.0, code license unverified.** Pattern-inspiration only. Do not vendor blind.
- **Optimal Weight / ISP (arXiv:2510.01499) — arXiv license, no code release.** Reimplement from the paper; the algorithm is described as small and deterministic.
- **Beta-Binomial posterior (arXiv:2601.13396) — arXiv license.** Reimplement from the paper.
- **NewsGuard / MBFC APIs — proprietary, closed, sovereignty-blocking.** Avoid. CRED-1 (CC BY 4.0) is the open substitute.
- **Sovereign-OK, commercial-OK, safe to vendor:** crowd-kit (Apache-2.0), scikit-learn (BSD-3), CRED-1 (CC BY 4.0, attribution required).

This confirms an important nuance for the critic report. The facet's headline "current" pick, OW/ISP (2510.01499), is a **multi-LLM-answer aggregation** method (aggregating multiple model outputs / crowd labels), *not* an evidence-source corroboration method. It's applied by analogy to POLARIS baskets (members = sources), which is defensible, but the facet over-states it as "the SOTA primitive for weight corroborators over a basket" without flagging that its native domain is LLM/crowd-vote aggregation, not multi-document evidence corroboration. That's a domain-transfer caveat the synthesis didn't carry. Notably its "real-world healthcare setting ARMMAN" is a maternal-health intervention, not clinical evidence aggregation.

I have enough verified material now. Let me write the completeness-critic findings. I'll be concise and severity-ordered, each with a primary-source URL, covering the four mandated hunt categories (a) missing methods, (b) repo-contradicting claims, (c) DNA violations, (d) dated-as-current.

# Completeness-Critic Findings — I-cons-001 Consolidation Landscape

I verified every load-bearing POLARIS claim against the repo myself (weight_mass.py, credibility_pass.py, corroboration.py, independence_collapse.py, calibration_metrics.py, finding_dedup.py, multi_section_generator.py, live_retriever.py) and ran primary-source web verification on the method claims. The synthesis is honest and mostly accurate — its self-flagged "only 1 of 7 facets supplied" caveat is correct and its default-on correction is verified. Below are the gaps it still has, severity-ordered.

## SEV-1 (highest) — Missing method that IS POLARIS's whole pipeline, published 2026

**GAP-1 — arXiv:2604.11036 "Uncertainty-Aware Web-Conditioned Scientific Fact-Checking" (13 Apr 2026, CC-BY-4.0) is missing and is the single closest published match to POLARIS's entire basket pipeline.** It does atomic predicate-argument decomposition → compact evidence-grounded per-fact checker → uncertainty-gated multi-source corroboration → abstain-with-NEI when retrieved evidence conflicts with context. That is, in order: POLARIS's atomization, strict_verify, the corroboration basket, and the "label-don't-silently-override" disclosure posture. It is more directly the SOTA-comparator-and-prior-art for I-cons-001 than any of the six sources the facet listed (which are crowd-voting, Bayesian-fidelity, or news-credibility papers reasoned-onto baskets by analogy). It belongs in Section 2 (set-level verification + corroboration steps) and Section 4 (bake-off / cite-as-prior-art). Primary source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.11036

## SEV-2 — Dated/mis-domained method presented as the headline "current" primitive

**GAP-2 — The facet's headline current pick, OW/ISP (arXiv:2510.01499), is a multi-LLM-answer / crowd-label aggregation method, not an evidence-source corroboration method, and the synthesis carries it forward without the domain-transfer caveat.** Verified against the abstract: title is "Beyond Majority Voting: LLM Aggregation by Leveraging Higher-Order Information"; it aggregates answers from multiple LLMs (evaluated on MMLU, UltraFeedback, and the ARMMAN maternal-health RL setting), not evidence sources backing one claim. Applying it to baskets (members = sources) is a legitimate analogy, but the synthesis states it as "the SOTA primitive for weight corroborators over a basket" with no flag that its native task is model-vote aggregation. A reviewer could crown it as the consolidation method when it is a transferred one. Demote to "pattern-transfer, domain-mismatched" and pair it with GAP-1's native-domain paper. Primary source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.01499

## SEV-2 — Whole missing facet (claim clustering) has named SOTA the synthesis left blank

**GAP-3 — The "claim clustering into baskets" facet, which the synthesis correctly flags as NOT RESEARCHED, has concrete named methods the report should at least name so the operator knows the gap is fillable.** POLARIS already does this clustering in code (independence_collapse TF-IDF cosine + claim_graph merge keys), but the report leaves the frontier comparator entirely blank. Two primary anchors: (a) **MultiClaimNet** (arXiv:2503.22280, 2025) — multilingual fact-checked claim-cluster dataset, the modern benchmark for "group same-claim items into one basket"; (b) **Proposition-Level Clustering for Multi-Document Summarization** (arXiv:2112.08770, NAACL 2022) — the foundational keep-all-members-then-verify-against-the-cluster method, directly analogous to POLARIS's keep-all basket + per-member isolated verify. Note 2112.08770 is 2022 — name it as **foundational, NOT current** (see GAP-6). Primary sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.22280 and https://arxiv.org/abs/2112.08770

**GAP-4 — The "conflict/contradiction detection" and "set-level verification" facets (both flagged NOT RESEARCHED) have a named 2026 SOTA the report omits: TRUST Agents (arXiv:2604.12184, 14 Apr 2026, CC-BY-4.0).** Its logic aggregator combines atomic verdicts with conjunction/disjunction/negation/implication — the explicit set-level / basket verdict primitive POLARIS approximates with its 4-value basket_verdict label. The paper itself names "uncertainty calibration" as the remaining bottleneck, which corroborates the synthesis's own ADD-4 (no calibrated basket confidence) from an independent source. Worth adding as pattern-only (no code released on the abstract page). Primary source: https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.12184

## SEV-3 — Repo-contradiction the synthesis did not catch (flag split)

**GAP-5 — The same flag name `PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN` defaults ON in the synthesis/generator path but OFF in the retriever path, and the synthesis only corrected the synthesis-path default.** I verified: credibility_pass.py:118, multi_section_generator.py:283/:7088, evidence_selector.py:1938 all default `os.getenv(..., "on")` → unset = ON. But live_retriever.py:379 reads `... in ("1","true","yes","on")` with default empty → unset = **OFF**. So on an unset (default) run, the down-weight-don't-drop redesign is LIVE in synthesis/selection but the retriever's content-starved/landing-page rows still take the legacy HARD-DROP path. This is a real split-brain the report should surface: the synthesis's blanket "credibility pass is default-on" is true for synthesis but the retrieval drop-vs-downweight half is default-off. (This is the same class of bug the credibility_pass.py:110-115 comment says caused the I-arch-007 602→22 funnel.) Code-grounded, no external source needed — primary source is the repo: `C:\POLARIS\src\polaris_graph\retrieval\live_retriever.py:372-381`.

## SEV-3 — Dated method risk (general guardrail, one concrete instance)

**GAP-6 — One source in the facet's foundation list, TruthFinder/CRH (arXiv:1505.02463), is a 2015 paper, and the proposition-clustering anchor I add in GAP-3 is 2022; neither may be crowned "current."** The facet labels TruthFinder honestly as "2015 survey, foundation still in active use," so this is not a violation by the facet — but the report's Section 1/2 must not present any pre-2024 method as the 2025/2026 best-practice recipe (per the frontier-tech-only / no-grandfather mandate). The OSS implementation (crowd-kit Dawid-Skene/M-MSR, Apache-2.0) IS current and is the right way to carry the 2015 idea forward, so the recommendation survives — but the framing must say "2015 concept, realized via the current crowd-kit impl," never "TruthFinder is the current method." Primary source: https://arxiv.org/abs/1505.02463 (crowd-kit: https://github.com/Toloka/crowd-kit)

## DNA / faithfulness-engine check — CLEAN

I checked every ADD/FIX recommendation against the weight-and-consolidate DNA and the frozen-engine rule. **No violation found.** All five ADD items (reliability-aware weighting, continuous correlation down-weight, credibility-weighted strength, Beta-Binomial confidence, wiring calibration_metrics) live in the advisory weight/label/confidence layers; none drops, caps, thins, or targets a breadth number; none touches strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance. The synthesis already carries the correct caution (Section 3, "any down-weight must be a disclosed weight, never a drop"). One thing to make explicit in the final report: ADD-2 (continuous correlation down-weight from OW/ISP) is the one most at risk of being mis-built as a thinner — it must lower a basket's *disclosed strength signal* only, never remove a member or gate a sentence, or it becomes a §-1.3-banned correlation-cap.

## Net

Of the four hunt categories: (a) **four missing methods** — GAP-1 (the big one), GAP-3, GAP-4; (b) **one repo-contradiction** the synthesis missed — GAP-5 (flag split-brain); (c) **DNA violations: none** — recommendations are clean, one framing caution noted; (d) **dated-as-current: none committed by the facet, one guardrail** — GAP-6. The synthesis's own headline honesty flag (6 of 7 facets unsupplied) stands and is the largest completeness gap of all — GAP-1/GAP-3/GAP-4 are precisely the named sources those missing facets should have produced.
